ASIC MAC Identity Architecture

This decision makes normalized MAC address the only durable identity for physical ASIC miners and ASIC work lanes across the BlockDAG pool stack.

Decision

Physical ASIC miners and ASIC work lanes are identified only as mac:<normalized-mac>. IP addresses, Stratum ports, worker names, wallet addresses, display names, and pool-log connection suffixes are observations or payout fields, not ASIC identities.

BDAG_ASIC_EXPECTED_MACS is the canonical expected physical ASIC lane list. The stack miner registry overlays that MAC-only list onto current observations, then projects current route-to-MAC observations into the pool through POOL_ASIC_MAC_OVERRIDES and passes the physical ASIC LAN scope through BDAG_ASIC_LAN_CIDRS. The pool uses that projection to expose active local ASIC lanes as MAC lanes. The dashboard consumes registry and pool MAC lane data; it must not invent a different ASIC identity.

Identity Contract

ConceptAllowed RoleNot Allowed
MAC addressDurable physical ASIC identity, BDAG_ASIC_EXPECTED_MACS lane entry, registry key, expected lane key, watchdog stall/cooldown key, earnings/history ASIC merge key.None for physical ASIC identity.
IP addressObserved route used to contact ASIC HTTP/Stratum endpoints and build the current POOL_ASIC_MAC_OVERRIDES projection. Remote Stratum clients outside BDAG_ASIC_LAN_CIDRS may use IP-based operational identity.ASIC registry key, dashboard identity key, expected work lane, watchdog state key, display suffix, earnings/history merge key for physical ASICs on the mining LAN.
Worker or walletPayout and credit attribution context.Physical ASIC identity or lane identity.
Stratum port or extranonce suffixCurrent connection/job correlation inside the bounded pool-log window.Durable ASIC identity or stale lane ownership after reconnect.
Display nameOptional operator label rendered with a MAC suffix.Identity key or uniqueness proof.

Unknown MAC

If a route in BDAG_ASIC_LAN_CIDRS cannot be resolved to a MAC address, do not promote it into a miner registry identity, work lane, earnings row, or watchdog state key. Count pool-log shares or blocks from that route as unattributed until the MAC is collected. The route may remain visible in diagnostics as observed_ip so an operator can find the device.

Control Plane Flow

  1. LAN discovery, ASIC API reads, neighbor table reads, and configured miner results collect current route observations.
  2. The stack registry stores physical ASIC records only when a MAC address is known, and marks entries in BDAG_ASIC_EXPECTED_MACS as managed lanes even if their route changes.
  3. pool_asic_mac_overrides_value() builds derived ip=mac projection entries from verified registry rows. This is a route map, not the authoritative lane list.
  4. Compose passes BDAG_ASIC_LAN_CIDRS into the pool so unresolved local ASIC routes cannot become IP lanes.
  5. The status sampler keeps POOL_ASIC_MAC_OVERRIDES in runtime env and recreates the pool container when the projection changes.
  6. The pool reports each ASIC connection as a MAC lane. The dashboard and watchdog consume those MAC identities.

Rejected Patterns

Do not add local ASIC fallbacks such as ip:<address>, inferred ASIC names from route addresses, expected-lane IDs from route addresses, or watchdog cooldown maps keyed by route address. Those patterns caused a live failure where multiple ASICs sharing one wallet were visible but useful work was attributed to only one apparent lane. The only IP identity exception is for remote Stratum clients outside the configured ASIC LAN.

Validation

PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest \
  ops/tests/test_miner_retirement_identity.py \
  ops/tests/test_status_sampler_mining_imperative.py \
  ops/tests/test_watchdog_miner_source_counts.py \
  ops/tests/test_mining_guard_30min.py \
  ops/tests/test_stack_naming_coherence.py

PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 scripts/validate-stack-defaults.py
BDAG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/bdag-stack-validate-runtime PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 scripts/validate-pi5-restart-hardening.sh
